title: Provider-Agnostic Client
description: MEAI's IChatClient lets you swap LLM providers by changing config, not code.

steps.annotation: Configuration selects the provider endpoint and model.

steps.content: Explain what a provider-agnostic chat client means.
steps.annotation: The user sends a message through the IChatClient abstraction.

steps.content: A provider-agnostic client wraps any LLM behind a single interface. You write code against IChatClient once and switch providers by changing configuration.
steps.annotation: The model responds through the same IChatClient regardless of which backend serves it.
Changing two config values routes all calls to a different provider.
The identical code path now reaches a different LLM backend.

steps.content: The IChatClient abstraction means your application code never changes when you switch providers. Only the connection details differ.
steps.annotation: Streaming, tool calls, and structured output all work identically across providers.
